name: Linode / Akamai Cloud API
what_it_does: Linode (now Akamai Cloud) provides a REST API for managing cloud compute (Linodes/VMs), managed Kubernetes (LKE), object storage, managed databases, firewalls, NodeBalancers (load balancers), and DNS. Developer-focused VPS with competitive pricing and a clean REST API at api.linode.com/v4.
use_cases: Provisioning Linode instances (VMs) and managing their lifecycle from agents
Scaling LKE (Linode Kubernetes Engine) clusters and node pools
Managing object storage buckets and objects programmatically
DNS record management for domains hosted on Linode
not_for: Teams requiring the breadth of managed services from AWS/GCP/Azure
Enterprise workloads requiring SOC1 or FedRAMP compliance
GPU-heavy ML workloads (limited GPU availability vs AWS)
Workloads requiring global presence across many regions
best_when: An agent needs affordable, reliable VPS infrastructure with a clean REST API and you don't need the complexity of hyperscaler ecosystems. Good for cost-sensitive compute and straightforward Kubernetes workloads.
avoid_when: You need advanced managed services (ML pipelines, advanced analytics, IoT), enterprise compliance certifications, or heavy cloud-native service integrations.

auth.notes: Personal Access Tokens with configurable expiry and fine-grained scopes (read-only or read-write per resource type: Linodes, LKE, object storage, etc.). OAuth2 for apps. PATs are the recommended auth method for agents — scope them to only the resource types the agent needs.

pricing.paid_tiers: Nanode (shared, 1GB RAM) from $5/month; Standard from $12/month (2GB RAM); LKE from $10/month/node; Object Storage $0.02/GB-month
pricing.notes: Competitive pricing, typically similar to DigitalOcean. Billing is hourly. API access is free. Credit card required. Akamai acquisition has not significantly changed pricing structure as of early 2026.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API returns structured JSON errors with errors array containing reason and field. Error messages are descriptive and reference the specific field or constraint violated. HTTP status codes are used correctly. Validation errors are especially clear.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Linode creation is not idempotent — duplicate names are allowed (multiple Linodes can share a label). Resource updates via PUT are idempotent. Agents must check existing resources before creating. LKE cluster operations are idempotent for node pool scaling.
known_agent_gotchas: Linode labels are not unique — agents can accidentally create duplicate instances; always query before creating
Most create operations are asynchronous — agents must poll resource status (provisioning -> running) before use
Rate limit of 800 RPM is generous but agents doing bulk provisioning must track and throttle
Object storage uses separate S3-compatible endpoints and credentials — not the main API token
LKE cluster config (kubeconfig) is only available after cluster reaches running state — poll before download
Resizing a Linode (disk resize) requires a reboot — agents must account for downtime
Webhooks not available — use polling for event-driven automation

security_score_components.security_notes: Personal Access Tokens support per-resource-type scopes (read-only vs read-write for Linodes, LKE, databases, etc.) — good granularity for least-privilege agents. HTTPS enforced. Object storage uses separate HMAC/S3-compatible credentials. No native secrets management — use external tools.
